summary: Phase 3 (contextual benchmark) + Phase 4 (visual sync history) tested end-to-end. Backend: 14/14 pytest passed (auth guards, fleet-percentile shape, benchmarks/history days clamping, sync-history shape/clamping, guardrails blocking for game and stream keywords, running_apps injection via /api/agent/report-specs). Frontend: login+navigation OK; /app/benchmark renders benchmark-card + benchmark-sparkline + silent-bench-btn. Guardrail flow works perfectly: injecting valorant.exe via agent token then clicking silent-bench-btn shows sonner warning toast 'Rilevati processi che falserebbero il benchmark - gioco attivo: valorant.exe' with 'Esegui comunque' and 'Annulla' actions; no immediate benchLaunch. fleet-percentile-card correctly hidden (fleet<3 users so available:false-ish state) and sync-timeline correctly hidden on empty health_history — both are expected per the spec.
critical_code_review_comments: benchmarks/history uses `int(days or 30)` which means days=0 silently falls back to 30 instead of clamping to 1. Not a bug per spec (spec says capped 1..90) but slightly surprising. Consider `int(days if days is not None else 30)` if strict 1..90 clamping is desired.
FleetPercentileCard fetches once on mount but ignores refreshKey prop passed from Benchmark.jsx (`key={fp-${refreshKey}}` on parent solves this via remount, so OK — just noting the pattern).
Guardrails endpoint response is well-shaped and severity levels match spec exactly (high/medium/info).
seed_data_creation: Temporarily injected running_apps=['valorant.exe'] / ['obs64.exe'] via /api/agent/report-specs during tests; final teardown resets running_apps=[] for admin.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoints /api/benchmarks/fleet-percentile, /api/benchmarks/guardrails, /api/benchmarks/history, /api/pc/sync-history all validated. Guardrail keywords include fortnite/valorant/cs2/obs64/streamlabs (see _GAME_KEYWORDS in pc.py). Injecting running_apps requires GET /api/agent/token first then POST /api/agent/report-specs with X-Agent-Token header. Frontend guardrail toast is a sonner warning with data-testid=bench-guardrail-toast (passed as a prop to toast opts).
